text:
Admission Date:  2007-06-09              Discharge Date:   1974-10-18

Date of Birth:  1985-01-02             Sex:   M

Service: MEDICINE

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Jaime
Chief Complaint:
chest and rib pain

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
cardiac catheterization 2007-06-09 s/p stent to LAD


History of Present Illness:
57 y/o man with h/o HTN presented to Telecare Santa Cruz Phf Hosp saying "I
think I may be having a heart attack."  The pt notes incr
belching, indigestion the past 2 days, with worsening
right-sided rib/chest pain and new substernal "rib pain" on deep
inspiration (pt is s/p rib fx from fall on 0946 Joshua Plains
North Ricky, TX 23018 railing 3
weeks ago).  He took some oxycodone without relief.  He says he
decr po intake and felt better.  1D PTA felt "woozy, sweaty"
several times, along with new substernal "rib pain" with deep
inspiration (an extension of his previous rib pain).  No
palpitations.  +SOB, with shallow breathing.  Says he talked to
a friend, whom he described his sx, and the friend suggested he
come in.  On arrival to Telecare Santa Cruz Phf Hospital, vitals BP: 116/82, P:
116.  EKG was done, showing NSR with ST segment elevation in
leads V1-V6 with Q waves V1-V3.  PT was given
ASA/Lovenox/Lopressor 5mg IV/NTG SL given X3 without relief of
pain.  Given morphine 2mg IV.  Transferred to Lakewood Health System for cath,
started on integrillin.  Taken to cath lab, admitted to CCU for
balloon pump placement for depressed CI and elevated filling
pressures.
Currently, CP remains the same, with decr fatigue, weakness
since yesterday.  Still has difficulty taking deep breath.


Past Medical History:
1.  HTN (ETT in past, WNL, 2000)
2.  rib fx 3 weeks ago

Social History:
Lives in Carl, was visiting [**State 3914**], works as a
painting contractor, smoked until 5 years ago, h/o smoking
1-2ppd for 20-25 years, h/o heavy alcohol use in past, quit 20
years ago, h/o IVDA, h/o cocaine use in the past.


Family History:
sister had MI at age 57, parents passed away in their 80s (no
heart ds)

Physical Exam:
57 y/o man.  Breathing comfortably.  NAD.  AOX3
HEENT:  PERRL, EOMI, MMM, no nasal discharge.
Neck:  Supple, Good ROM, JVD at 6cm
Lungs:  With fine crackles at the left base. Otherwise CTAB
CV:  RRR S1 and S2 audible. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
Abd:  Soft, NT, ND. NABS. No masses.  No HSM.
Peripheral:  Trace edema.  Warm skin.  No cyanosis, clubbing.
2+ symmetric pulses distally, dorsalis pedis and posterior
tibial arteries.
Neuro:  No focal deficits.  CN 2-12 intact.  Senstory 03-14
throughout.  Motor 03-14 throughout.  Gait not assessed.

Pertinent Results:
Labs:OSH
tbil 0.8
alk phos 137
AST 464
ALT 198
lipase 187
CK 1343, TnI pending, MB pending
.
1:30am 2007-06-09
CK: 1342  MB: Pnd  Trop-*T*: Pnd
.
EKG: SR at 99, St elevation in V1-V6, I, aVL, II c/w mid-distal
LAD lesion Q waves in V1-V6 with TWIs c/w evolving infarct
.
Cardiac Enzyme PEAKS
2007-06-09 6:44A CK 1490*
2007-06-09 1:23A CK-MB 118* and TnT: GREATER THAN 25
CTROPNT > 0.10 NG/ML SUGGESTS ACUTE MI
.
CATH 2006-01-12
CO 2.86, CI 1.38
AO (S/D/M) 82/65/67
PCW (M/A/V) 34/31/38
RA (M/A/V) 18/21/20
PA (S/D/M) 41/27/34
RV (S/D/M) 41/16/19
LMCA: normal
LAD: occluded after D1, no collaterals
LCX: normal
RCA: normal
LAD occlusion crossed without restoration of flow which was
reestablished after export thrombectomy.  Occlusion stented with
Cypher stent.  After intracoronary NTG, no residual, normal
flow.  Because of markedly elevated filling pressures and low
cardiac output and SBP 80-90, IABP placed.
COMMENTS:
1. Selective coronary angiography showed a right dominant system
with
one vessel disease. The LMCA was without disease
angiographically. The
LAD was occluded after the first diagonal branch in its mid
portion.
There were no collaterals filling the distal vessel. The LCX was
without
flow limiting stenoses. The RCA was without flow limiting
stenoses.
2. limited resting hemodynamics showed moderate pulmonary
hypertension
(PA mean 35 mmHg). The left and right sided filling pressures
were
severely elevated (RVEDP 22 mmHg, PCW mean 33 mmHg). The cardiac
output
was depressed (CO 2.9 l/min, CI 1.4 l/min/m2).
3. Successful thrombectomy, PTCA and stenting of the mid LAD
with a 2.5
x 13 mm Cypher DES which was postdilated to 2.75 mm proximally.
Final
angiography revealed no residual stenosis, no apparent
dissection, and
normal epicardial flow (see PTCA comments).
4. Successful insertion of an 8 French 40 cc intraaortic balloon
pump
with good augmentation and unloading.
FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
1. One vessel coronary artery disease.
2. Subacute anterior myocardial infarction managed by primary
PCI and
deployment of a drug-eluting stent in the mid LAD.
3. Successful balloon pump placement.
4. Severe diastolic dysfunction.
5. Moderate pulmonary hypertension.
.
Echo: 2007-06-09: EF 30-35%
The LA is normal in size. LV wall thicknesses and cavity size
are normal. There is moderate regional LV systolic dysfunction
with akinesis of the distal half of the anterior septum and
anterior walls. The apex is mildly aneurysmal and dyskinetic.
The distal inferior wall is severely hypokinetic. No masses or
thrombi are seen in the left ventricle. RV chamber size and free
wall motion arenormal. The aortic root, ascending aorta and
aortic arch are mildly dilated. The AV leaflets (3) appear
structurally normal with good leaflet excursion and no aortic
regurgitation. The mitral valve appears structurally normal with
trivial mitral regurgitation. There is no mitral valve prolapse.
The estimated PA systolic pressure is normal. No pericardial
effusion.
.
1993-07-13 1:20 am URINE

                            **FINAL REPORT 1974-10-18**

   URINE CULTURE (Final 1974-10-18):
      ENTEROCOCCUS SP..    >100,000 ORGANISMS/ML..

                              SENSITIVITIES: MIC expressed in
MCG/ML

_________________________________________________________
                             ENTEROCOCCUS SP.
                             |
AMPICILLIN------------   <=2 S
LEVOFLOXACIN----------     1 S
NITROFURANTOIN--------  <=16 S
VANCOMYCIN------------   <=1 S


Brief Hospital Course:
Impression:
57 y/o man with h/o HTN (other CAD risk factors-smoking, FH+),
presented with ST segment elevation myocardial infarction,
status post cardiac catheterization showing one vessel coronary
artery disease, subacute anterior myocardial infarction managed
by primary PCI and deployment of a drug-eluting stent in the mid
LAD, and complicated by depressed CO and increased PCWP
requiring intra-aortic balloon pump placement.
1.  Cardiac
*Coronary:  Post-cath, the pt was begun on aspirin, plavix,
integrillin drip for 18 hours and a statin.  He was weaned off
the intra-aortic balloon pump successfully.  His hospital course
was complicated by residual chest pain, despite reperfusion
being successful, with evidence of prolonged infarct on EKGs.
This chest pain was difficult to assess as the pt sustained
several rib fractures weeks ago, and had residual
musculoskeletal chest pain because of this.  His pain was
managed well with oxycodone, and at discharge the pt was chest
pain-free.  An ACEI and BB was added to his medication regimen
post-cath when his blood pressure and HR tolerated them, and
these medications were titrated up to optimal blood pressure
control.
*Pump:  With depressed cardiac output and increased PCWP in the
cath lab, although the pt had no clinical evidence of failure.
As stated above, the pt was weaned off IABP successfully.  His
Echocardiogram showed an EF of 30-35% with moderate regional
left ventricular systolic dysfunction with akinesis of the
distal half of the anterior septum and anterior walls. The apex
is mildly aneurysmal and dyskinetic. The distal inferior wall is
severely hypokinetic. He was subsequently started on coumadin po
and lovenox injections, and his INR will be followed as an
outpatient.  He is to have his INR checked two days post
discharge, with results being called to Dr. Taylor or Dr.
Hester.  He plans to then travel back to Carl.  An
appointment was set up for him with his primary care physician,
Tyler Kelly also with a cardiologist in Carl.  The pt was
given his echo report and cath report to bring to his doctors.

*Rhythm:  Normal sinus rhythm, monitored on telemetry throughout
his stay without significant arrhythmia.
2.  Urinary Tract Infection:  The patient developed a
temperature during his stay and was pan-cultured.  He did not
have any symptoms.  The patient did have a foley during his
admission.  Blood cultures showed no growth X 4 bottles.  Urine
cultures grew out positive Enterococcus, sensitive to
Amoxicillin.  The pt was discharged on amoxicillin and will
complete a full 7 day course of antibiotics.  He received one
day of vancomycin before his culture results came back sensitive
to Amoxicillin, at which time his abx were changed.
3.  Rib pain:  secondary to rib fractures the pt sustained prior
to admission by falling on a railing near his home.  He was
started on his usual dose of oxycodone with morphine IV
supplemented his first night.  He was then transitioned to his
usual dose of oxycodone as an outpatient.
4.  Indigestion
The pt was given a proton pump inhibitor po and maalox prn
during his stay with good control.
5.  FULL CODE

Medications on Admission:
Meds:
1.  Norvasc
2.  Indocin (indomethacin)
3.  Cardizem 360mg
4.  Oxycodone 5mg po qd


Discharge Medications:
1. Aspirin 325 mg Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) Sig: One (1)
Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.) PO DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet, Delayed Release (E.C.)(s)* Refills:*2*
2. Clopidogrel 75 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY (Daily)
for 30 days.
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*8*
3. Atorvastatin Calcium 80 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO
DAILY (Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
4. Amoxicillin 500 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO Q12H
(every 12 hours) for 7 days.
Disp:*14 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*
5. Oxycodone 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO Q4H (every 4
hours) as needed.
Disp:*40 Tablet(s)* Refills:*0*
6. Metoprolol Succinate 50 mg Tablet Sustained Release 24HR Sig:
One (1) Tablet Sustained Release 24HR PO once a day.
Disp:*30 Tablet Sustained Release 24HR(s)* Refills:*3*
7. Lisinopril 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO once a day.
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*3*
8. Warfarin Sodium 5 mg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO QHS (once
a day (at bedtime)).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
9. Outpatient Lab Work
Please check PT, PTT, and INR.  Call the results to Dr. Elizabeth
Thornton, MD, Cardiologist, pager:  b2400283-9bbe-5e1a-6560-098f5ac14e16.
10. Lovenox 100 mg/mL Solution Sig: One (1)  Subcutaneous twice
a day.
Disp:*10 injections* Refills:*2*


Discharge Disposition:
Home

Discharge Diagnosis:
1.  ST segment elevation myocardial infarction status post stent
to the left anterior descending artery
2.  Urinary tract infection
3.  status post rib fractures


Discharge Condition:
Stable


Discharge Instructions:
If you experience any chest pain, shortness of breath, or
sweating, please report to the ER immediately.
Please take all of your medications.
Please follow up with your doctors (see information below).
Do NOT stop taking your aspirin or plavix under any
circumstances until your cardiologist tells you to.


Followup Instructions:
1.  Follow up with a cardiologist within 1-2 weeks.  You will
need to have your INR checked to monitor your coumadin levels.
2.  You should attend cardiac rehab when you return.
3.  Please follow up with Dr. Cruz in Carl in his
clinic.  Your appointment is for Wednesday, 2008-01-19,
at 8:30am.
4.  Please come to the ICU Lab on Thursday in the AM.  They
will call Dr. Taylor or Dr. Hester with the results.



Completed by:1982-10-09
